summary.intent: Fix a git merge conflict issue caused by committing a minified (single-line) CSS file, by switching to unminified Tailwind output so the file is diffable.
summary.outcome: Rebuilt CSS without --minify flag (3805 lines, 92KB), updated build.sh to remove --minify, updated CLAUDE.md to document the change, and committed the unminified style.css.

repo: style.css is committed to the repo (not gitignored) so that cargo build works without requiring Node.js
CSS assets are embedded via include_dir! macro in the release build
Tailwind build script lives at crates/web/ui/build.sh
CLAUDE.md documents the step to commit style.css after Tailwind rebuilds

code.finding: Removed --minify flag from the Tailwind build command to produce diffable multi-line output
Updated to note the build change and commit step after Tailwind rebuilds

workflow: Minified CSS (single-line output) causes merge conflicts on every concurrent branch touching Tailwind classes — committing unminified output avoids this
Browsers gzip CSS on the wire, so skipping --minify has negligible size impact for dev/CI
The Tailwind rebuild command changes the working directory, requiring care when checking file paths from repo root

friction: cd side-effect from Tailwind build command caused confusion when checking line counts — had to re-run from repo root
One-time large diff when switching from minified (1 line) to unminified (3805 lines) — noisy but expected
open_items: Verify that the non-minified CSS doesn't noticeably impact production bundle size or load times for users
